articles.title: She was told she might never walk again. Now she’s running Boston.
articles.description:
After learning to walk again at Spaulding, Caylie McMahon is running Boston alongside lifelong friend Lindsey Stagg to give back to the place that changed her life.

The post She was told she might never walk again. Now she’s running Boston. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Caylie McMahon, once told she may never walk again, is now running the Boston Marathon.
She is participating alongside lifelong friend Lindsey Stagg to honor Spaulding Rehabilitation.
Their journey inspires Boston residents about resilience and the impact of local rehabilitation services.

articles.title: Patriots opt for line help in latest NFL mock draft
articles.description:
"Banks' power and push in the middle could open things further for [Christian] Barmore and [Milton] Williams to rush the passer."

The post Patriots opt for line help in latest NFL mock draft appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Patriots prioritize offensive line help in latest NFL mock draft.
Drafting Banks could enhance pass rush for Barmore and Williams.
Improved line play may boost Patriots' performance for Boston fans.

articles.title: Retired Scituate couple mourned after fatal helicopter crash
articles.description:
Margaret Rimmler and Patrick Haskell died last week after a sightseeing helicopter went down off the coast of Kauai.

The post Retired Scituate couple mourned after fatal helicopter crash appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Margaret Rimmler and Patrick Haskell died in a helicopter crash off Kauai last week.
The couple was retired and lived in Scituate, Massachusetts.
Their tragic loss has deeply affected the Scituate community and local residents.

articles.title: Mass. school bus driver fired after allegedly urinating inside bus with students onboard
articles.description:
Officials say the 71-year-old driver was on his way to an elementary school with 12 pre-K to second-grade students.

The post Mass. school bus driver fired after allegedly urinating inside bus with students onboard appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: A 71-year-old bus driver was fired for urinating inside a bus with students onboard.
The incident involved 12 pre-K to second-grade students on their way to school.
Parents express concern over student safety and call for stricter hiring practices.

articles.title: More investment in women’s hockey means players peak later. What does that mean for this year’s PWHL draft class?
articles.description:
As college players improve later into their careers, a 2026 prospect class — led by Olympic gold medalists — reflects the sport’s rapid growth and rising talent ceiling.

The post More investment in women’s hockey means players peak later. What does that mean for this year’s PWHL draft class? appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Investment in women's hockey leads to improved player performance over time.
The 2026 PWHL draft class features Olympic gold medalists as top prospects.
Boston residents can expect a stronger local women's hockey scene in upcoming years.

articles.title: Founder of ‘orgasmic meditation’ company gets 9 years in prison in forced labor conspiracy
articles.description:
"Coercion disguised as wellness or empowerment is still exploitation."

The post Founder of ‘orgasmic meditation’ company gets 9 years in prison in forced labor conspiracy appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Founder of a Boston-based orgasmic meditation company sentenced to 9 years for forced labor conspiracy.
Company reportedly exploited individuals under the guise of wellness and empowerment.
Local advocates emphasize need for awareness on coercion disguised as wellness in Boston.

articles.title: Trump unveils renderings of planned presidential library tower
articles.description:
Trump’s planned library would be notably different than his predecessors’ facilities, particularly in its size and scope.

The post Trump unveils renderings of planned presidential library tower appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Trump reveals designs for a presidential library tower, differing significantly from previous libraries.
The library's size and scope aim to attract visitors and boost local tourism in Boston.
Construction could create jobs and economic opportunities for Boston residents in the coming years.
